plainEnglish: At Level AAA, if your text requires reading ability beyond roughly the lower-secondary (about 9th-grade) level, provide a simpler alternative or supplemental content such as summaries or illustrations. Aim to write plainly so more people can understand.
whyItMatters: Users with reading disabilities or limited literacy can't understand content written above a lower-secondary reading level without a simpler alternative.
quickCheck: Run your text through a readability checker (e.g., Hemingway or a Flesch–Kincaid tool); if it's above ~9th-grade level, provide a plain-language summary.
commonMistakes: Dense, jargon-heavy prose with no plain-language summary.
Long, complex sentences where short ones would do.
No summaries, illustrations, or supplemental content for difficult material.

codeExamples.label: Plain-language summary alongside the detail
codeExamples.code:
In short: you can cancel any time, no fee.

Subscribers may terminate the agreement at their sole discretion without incurring early-termination penalties...

codeExamples.label: Dense legal prose with no simpler version
codeExamples.code: Subscribers may terminate the agreement at their sole discretion without incurring early-termination penalties...
